compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on economic disparity, all lowercase
response:
# **the gap**

verse 1:
born on the wrong side of the zip code line
where the schools are broken and the water ain't fine
mama working doubles just to keep the lights on
by the time she gets home, half the night is gone

pre-chorus:
they say pull yourself up, yeah they love that phrase
from their gated houses and their trust fund haze
easy words to say when you never fell down
when you never watched them auction off your town

chorus:
there's a mountain made of money
and we're living in the valley below
they keep moving up the ladder
pulling it up so we can never follow
the gap keeps getting wider
like a river we can't cross
and they're counting all their billions
while we're counting what we lost

verse 2:
kid on the corner got a dream in his chest
but the system keeps on putting him to the test
no connections, no collateral, no safety net
just a pile of student loans and a mountain of debt

pre-chorus:
they say the market will fix it, just be patient and wait
but patience don't pay rent and patience don't feed plates
the invisible hand never reached down here
just took what little we had year after year

*(chorus)*

bridge:
what does it cost to be born without a name
that opens doors and plays the generational game
we didn't write the rules but we live inside them
we didn't build these walls but we're trapped behind them

outro:
the gap keeps getting wider
somebody tell me when enough is enough
the gap keeps getting wider
and silence never changed a single thing
